medical_history.chief_complaint: presence of asymptomatic rough, diffuse, white patches of unknown etiology and progress, located bilaterally in the oral mucosa, mucosa of the upper lip, gum and ventral surface of the tongue
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Lesions appeared in adolescence, asymptomatic, no systemic changes reported
medical_history.family_history: Adolescents' mother had similar lesions in her mouth, suggesting autosomal dominant disorder

physical_examination.specialty_examination: White, corrugated, diffuse plaques in oral mucosa, mucosa of upper lip, gum, and ventral surface of tongue; cytology showed perinuclear eosinophilic condensation of epithelial cells; histopathology showed hyperparakeratosis and acanthosis with vacuolization in keratinocytes in suprabasal layers
